author: Ameen Hassan
summary: This session was a queue operation invoking the session memory extractor on a prior conversation transcript. The input transcript was itself a recursive queue operation — a memory extraction pass processing a prior session that was also a memory extraction pass. The AgentOS SessionStart hook successfully loaded project context at session open. The assistant identified the recursive nature of the input and produced a valid JSON memory object reflecting the meta-role of that session. No new code was written and no new decisions were made in this session itself. The session confirms that the queue-operation pattern for session memory extraction is functioning and self-consistent, including handling of recursively nested extraction jobs.
problems: Input transcript was a recursive queue operation (memory extractor processing a prior memory extraction session), requiring identification of the meta-role rather than treating it as a standard feature session
approaches: Identified recursive structure of input transcript and produced JSON reflecting the meta-role of the prior session rather than treating it as a substantive feature session
results: Valid JSON memory object produced for a recursively nested queue operation
AgentOS SessionStart hook confirmed loading context successfully at session open
Queue-operation pattern shown to be self-consistent when applied to its own outputs

decisions.decision: Treat recursive queue operations as valid sessions with summary reflecting their meta-role

open_questions: Whether the canonical summary update from prior sessions was actually persisted successfully in the MCP server
